title: Weekly AI Governance Briefing: Disability Discrimination in Hiring and Race Corrections in Clinical Algorithms
key_themes: Systemic algorithmic bias in automated employment screening systems under the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA).
Phasing out of race-based correction factors in clinical pulmonary function testing algorithms.
legal_commercial_takeaways: Joint federal enforcements indicate that hiring platforms and automated screening tools must provide accessible, alternative evaluation methods for candidates with disabilities.
Relying on hiring software vendor claims of compliance does not insulate employers from regulatory liability under the ADA.
Race-based algorithmic correction variables in healthcare are facing intense regulatory deprecation as medical guidelines align to eliminate racial corrections.
governance_lessons: Mandate rigorous, documented vendor questionnaires reviewing ADA compliance, alternative assessments, and accessibility support.
Implement clinical validation processes to verify that healthcare software uses objective biological and clinical variables rather than crude racial categories.
evidence_actions: Obtain and log standard VPAT (Voluntary Product Accessibility Template) documents for all third-party automated hiring software.
Formally log validation audit reports checking that local spirometry and clinical diagnostic equipment runs software with race-free diagnostic guidelines.
source_note: This digest compiles risk intelligence from official US DOJ and EEOC public guidelines and American Thoracic Society clinical consensus guidelines. No external or unverified third-party content is replicated.
